message.content:
I need to understand the playwright test mocking pattern used in the public-pages app at /Users/duhl/git/ui/apps/public-pages. Specifically:

1. How are server-to-server API requests mocked in playwright tests (not browser-to-server)?
2. Find example test files that demonstrate this mocking pattern
3. Look at playwright config files
4. Look at any mock server setup or fixtures

Please read the relevant files and show me the actual code patterns used. Focus on: how mocks are defined, how tests send/register mocks, and what the mock server infrastructure looks like. Be thorough - show the actual code.

content.text: Perfect! Let me read the mock setup files:

content.content:
1	import { fileURLToPath, URL } from "node:url";
2	import tailwindcss from "@tailwindcss/vite";
3	import { devtools } from "@tanstack/devtools-vite";
4	import { tanstackStart } from "@tanstack/react-start/plugin/vite";
5	import viteReact from "@vitejs/plugin-react";
6	import { nitro } from "nitro/vite";
7	import { defineConfig } from "vite";
8	import viteTsConfigPaths from "vite-tsconfig-paths";
9	
10	const config = defineConfig(() => {
11	  const isVitest = process.env.VITEST === "true";
12	  const isDev = process.env.NODE_ENV === "development";
13	  const tanstackDevtoolsPort = Number.parseInt(
14	    process.env.TANSTACK_DEVTOOLS_PORT ?? "42069",
15	    10,
16	  );
17	  const devHmrHost = process.env.VITE_DEV_HMR_HOST;
18	  const devHmrClientPort = process.env.VITE_DEV_HMR_CLIENT_PORT;
19	  const devHmrProtocol = process.env.VITE_DEV_HMR_PROTOCOL;
20	
21	  return {
22	    plugins: [
23	      ...(isDev
24	        ? [
25	            devtools({
26	              eventBusConfig: {
27	                port: tanstackDevtoolsPort,
28	              },
29	            }),
30	          ]
31	        : []),
32	      // this is the plugin that enables path aliases
33	      viteTsConfigPaths({
34	        projects: ["./tsconfig.json"],
35	      }),
36	      tailwindcss(),
37	      tanstackStart(),
38	      viteReact(),
39	      ...(isVitest
40	        ? []
41	        : [
42	            nitro({
43	              // Work around a nitro-nightly externals bug with packages that
44	              // contain null branches in `exports` (e.g. msw/interceptors).
45	              noExternals: true,
46	            }),
47	          ]),
48	    ],
49	    server: {
50	      allowedHosts: ["anedot.me", "public.anedot.me", "localhost"],
51	      cors: true,
52	      ...(devHmrHost
53	        ? {
54	            hmr: {
55	              clientPort: devHmrClientPort
56	                ? Number.parseInt(devHmrClientPort, 10)
57	                : 443,
58	              host: devHmrHost,
59	              overlay: true,
60	              protocol: devHmrProtocol ?? "wss",
61	              timeout: 60000,
62	            },
63	          }
64	        : {}),
65	      host: true,
66	      port: 3207,
67	      strictPort: true,
68	      watch: {
69	        ignored: [
70	          "*.log",
71	          ".DS_Store",
72	          ".claude/**",
73	          ".git/**",
74	          ".github/**",
75	          ".output/**",
76	          ".tanstack/**",
77	          ".turbo/**",
78	          ".vscode/**",
79	          "dist/**",
80	          "node_modules/**",
81	          "playwright-report/**",
82	          "tests/**",
83	        ],
84	      },
85	    },
86	    test: {
87	      environment: "jsdom",
88	      globals: true,
89	      setupFiles: ["@workspace/test-utils/vitest"],
90	      passWithNoTests: true,
91	      exclude: [
92	        "**/node_modules/**",
93	        "**/dist/**",
94	        "**/.{idea,git,cache,output,temp}/**",
95	        "**/playwright-report/**",
96	        "**/test-results/**",
97	        "tests/integration/**",
98	        "**/*.spec.*",
99	      ],
100	    },
101	    optimizeDeps: {
102	      exclude: [
103	        "msw",
104	        "@mswjs/interceptors",
105	        "@tanstack/start-server-core",
106	        "@tanstack/start-client-core",
107	        "@tanstack/react-start",
108	      ],
109	    },
110	    resolve: {
111	      alias: {
112	        "@": fileURLToPath(new URL("./src", import.meta.url)),
113	      },
114	    },
115	  };
116	});
117	
118	export default config;
119
